name: Waydev Engineering Analytics API
what_it_does: Engineering analytics platform with REST API — enabling engineering leaders to analyze Git activity, measure DORA metrics, track developer performance trends, identify code quality risks, and benchmark teams against industry standards, with integrations into GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, and Jira.
use_cases: Tracking DORA metrics via Waydev API for engineering delivery performance reporting
Analyzing Git contribution patterns via Waydev API for team productivity insights
Measuring code review cycle time via Waydev API for PR bottleneck identification
Benchmarking engineering teams via Waydev API against industry performance standards
Generating engineering reports via Waydev API for VP/director leadership dashboards
not_for: Individual developer surveillance for performance reviews without ethical framework
Teams not using GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or Azure DevOps
best_when: An engineering director needs Git-based analytics with flexible multi-source integration (GitHub + GitLab + Jira) and industry benchmarking to contextualize team performance.
avoid_when: You have concerns about developer surveillance culture, need real-time monitoring, or prefer developer-positive metrics platforms.

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Waydev account settings. REST API at api.waydev.co. GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure DevOps OAuth for data ingestion. Jira integration for issue correlation. No webhooks. Read-only analytics API.

pricing.notes: Romanian company (Bucharest). Founded 2018 by Alex Circei and Andrei Iosif. Bootstrapped to profitability. Waydev has a broad developer analytics approach including individual contributor metrics — more controversial than Swarmia or LinearB. Git analytics: commits, PRs, code volume, churn, impact. DORA metrics: deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR. Jira correlation: ticket velocity, estimation accuracy. Industry benchmarks: percentile rankings. Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Jira, Slack, PagerDuty. Individual metrics: coding days, active days, new work, legacy refactor — flagged for surveillance concerns.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer token. GitHub/GitLab/Azure DevOps required. Individual metrics (surveillance risk). Romanian company (GDPR). No webhooks. Read-only analytics. Monthly contracts available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All metric reads are idempotent — historical Git analytics data is deterministic. Team and contributor reads idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Individual developer metrics included by default — configure carefully to avoid surveillance misuse
Read-only API — Git integrations must be configured in UI before API data available
Romanian company — EU GDPR compliance; EU data residency by default
No webhooks — poll API for data refresh; no event-driven architecture
Industry benchmarking uses Waydev's customer dataset — verify statistical validity
Multiple data sources (GitHub + Jira) require separate OAuth integrations in UI

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token API key. GDPR compliant (Romanian company, EU data residency). Individual developer data (commit patterns, PR activity, coding time) is sensitive — labor law implications in some jurisdictions. GitHub/GitLab OAuth credentials must be secured. No webhook secrets. Read-only analytics reduces breach impact.
